The forum's strongest publishable project: a data-rich study showing that political crises crowd out routine legislation through committee bottlenecks, with a theoretically sharp conditional mechanism that depends on the ruling party's seat share.

Committee-based livelihood classification (5 committees, N=6,079) shows passage rate decline of -20.4pp (34.5% to 14.1%) after Dec 3, with DID vs non-livelihood of -4.8pp (z=-3.28, p=0.001). But vs 'Other' committees excluding 법사위/재정위, DID is only -1.2pp (p=0.456) - not significant.

Committee-based and keyword-based livelihood classifications agree on 74.1% of bills (kappa=0.366). Committee method captures 6,079 bills vs keyword method's 3,183. Both show directionally consistent passage rate declines. Committee-based DID (-4.8pp) is smaller than keyword-based DID (-8.6pp).

재정경제기획위원회 passage rate rose from 25.7% to 28.4% (+2.7pp) after Dec 3, while all other committee groups declined 17-20pp. This anomaly drives the livelihood-vs-all DID and is the strongest evidence of selective processing within the general freeze.

Dec 2024-Jan 2025 committee meeting count was 144% of historical baseline (55 vs mean 38.2). The legislature held MORE meetings, not fewer. The bottleneck was in bill passage, not in meeting convening. Committees used meeting time for crisis deliberation rather than routine bill processing.

Seasonal adjustment reduces the estimated crisis effect by roughly half. Normal Dec-Jan penalty is -3.9pp (livelihood) and -5.5pp (non-livelihood). The 22nd Assembly's penalty was -11.2pp and -11.9pp respectively. Crisis excess: livelihood -7.3pp (z=-3.44), non-livelihood -6.4pp (z=-5.15). Differential is only -0.9pp.

After seasonal adjustment, the livelihood-specific penalty disappears. Oct-Nov to Dec-Jan comparison shows crisis excess of -3.8pp (livelihood, z=-2.66) and -3.9pp (non-livelihood, z=-1.99). Differential is +0.1pp - essentially zero. The crisis effect is uniform across committee types.

Within December 2024, committee activity dipped sharply on Dec 4-5 (56 events on Dec 5 vs 502 on Dec 3) before rebounding from Dec 6 onward. The crisis caused a 2-3 day freeze, not a sustained shutdown. Dec 17-20 saw 1,102 events across 5 meetings.

Three livelihood committees (보건복지, 교육, 농림축산식품해양수산) show nearly identical passage rate declines of 25-26pp. 기후에너지환경노동위원회 is an outlier with only -1.6pp, likely because it was newly formed in the 22nd Assembly with slow pre-crisis ramp-up.

A robust, multi-specification null finding that Korean legislators' real estate wealth does not predict housing bill sponsorship is itself a publishable contribution - it identifies a scope condition for self-interest theories of legislative behavior.
